title: Mexico City Urban Quality Index
description: Alcaldía-level UQI components by decade, parsed from DataMexCity.xlsx.

variables.label: Population
variables.description: Number of residents living in each current alcaldía.

variables.label: Population density
variables.description: Population divided by current alcaldía area; treated as pressure/crowding for the default UQI.

variables.label: Transit access
variables.description: Weighted transit station count normalized by population.

variables.label: Persons per dwelling
variables.description: Crowding indicator; lower values score better.

variables.label: Electricity coverage
variables.description: Share of inhabited dwellings with electricity.

variables.label: Piped water coverage
variables.description: Share of inhabited dwellings with piped water.

variables.label: Drainage coverage
variables.description: Share of inhabited dwellings with drainage.

variables.label: Green space per capita
variables.description: Current SEDEMA green-space totals divided by historical population.

variables.label: Third spaces
variables.description: Museums and public libraries per 100,000 residents.

sourceNotes.population: Number of residents living in the territory of each current alcaldía. Source: INEGI Censos + historical reconstructions.
sourceNotes.density: Density = Population ÷ Alcaldía area in km². Area held constant at present-day values (consistent geography across decades).
sourceNotes.transit_access: Weighted count of transit stations within the alcaldía, normalized by population. Modes weighted by capacity/service quality (Metro=1.00 benchmark).
sourceNotes.persons_per_dwelling: Household size / crowding indicator. Lower values typically indicate wealthier alcaldías. CDMX-wide average dropped from ~5.5 (1900) to 3.3 (2020).
sourceNotes.dwellings: Population ÷ persons-per-dwelling, rounded. The 2020 column matches INEGI's published per-alcaldía counts within 0.1–1.4%.
sourceNotes.electricity: Universal coverage citywide by 2020 (99.9%). Inequality is HISTORICAL — when did each alcaldía get electrified?
sourceNotes.piped_water: NOT universal even in 2020: Iztapalapa at ~62%, with many residents depending on pipas. The indicator with the most present-day inequality.
sourceNotes.drainage: Universal (99.7%) citywide by 2020. Rural alcaldías only reached near-universal coverage in the 2000s.
sourceNotes.hospital_access: Major hospital-level institutions only (public, IMSS, ISSSTE, national institutes, major private hospitals). Small clinics and centros de salud excluded.
sourceNotes.green_space: Current SEDEMA green-space totals divided by historical population — shows how m² per capita evolved as population grew.
sourceNotes.third_spaces: Cumulative count of museums and public libraries with documented opening dates, divided by population.

method.normalization: Min-max by selected variable within the active decade.
method.aggregation: Equal-weight average of selected normalized variables.
